programme.title: Tamron Hall
programme.desc: Two moms open up about the guilt they feel for not always wanting to play with their children; certified conscious parenting coach Reem Raouda shares how parents can stay present with their kids without losing themselves; psychologist Chanda Reynolds discusses how kids can preserve their individuality while sharing rooms with siblings; public health educator Dorian Johnson on raising resilient, healthy kids.

programme.title: Live: NBA Basketball
programme.desc: The Nuggets face the Timberwolves at Target Center for Game 4. Minnesota leads 2-1 after a dominant Game 3 where they held Denver to a record 11 first-quarter points. Ayo Dosunmu sparked the bench with a game-high 25 points and nine assists.

programme.title: Live: NBA Basketball
programme.desc: The Nuggets face the Timberwolves at Target Center for Game 4. Minnesota leads 2-1 after a dominant Game 3 where they held Denver to a record 11 first-quarter points. Ayo Dosunmu sparked the bench with a game-high 25 points and nine assists.

programme.title: Live: Inside the NBA
programme.desc: This Emmy Award-winning studio show, which airs following NBA telecasts on ESPN, is hosted by Ernie Johnson, with analysis from Hall of Famer Charles Barkley and NBA champions Kenny Smith and Shaquille O'Neal. It's a mix of highlights, interviews and hijinks, notably the verbal sparring sessions between the outspoken Barkley and his straight man, Smith. The show also welcomes various guest analysts throughout the season.

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Created in 1997 by veteran journalist Barbara Walters, "The View" is a daytime talk show hosted by women -- including Whoopi Goldberg, Joy Behar, Sunny Hostin, Sara Haines, Alyssa Farah Griffin and Ana Navarro -- and each offers her take on the day's news during the opening "Hot Topics" segment. Later, the ladies welcome various celebrities, who join them in a chat or perform for the audience. The program also offers tips on beauty, fashion, diet and relationships. Known for their freewheeling style, the hosts are often lampooned in late-night sketches.

programme.title: George Poveromo's World of Saltwater Fishing
programme.desc: Renowned saltwater fishing master George Poveromo hosts this long-running series that presents information on catching a wide range of coastal and offshore saltwater game fish. Filming occurs along the coastal U.S. and popular angling destinations abroad, with Poveromo providing tips on how to catch a wide variety of fish, from exotic species like blue marlin and bonefish to regional favorites such as dolphin, wahoo, tuna, king mackerel, striped bass, grouper, snapper, bluefish, redfish and flounder.

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Created in 1997 by veteran journalist Barbara Walters, "The View" is a daytime talk show hosted by women -- including Whoopi Goldberg, Joy Behar, Sunny Hostin, Sara Haines, Alyssa Farah Griffin and Ana Navarro -- and each offers her take on the day's news during the opening "Hot Topics" segment. Later, the ladies welcome various celebrities, who join them in a chat or perform for the audience. The program also offers tips on beauty, fashion, diet and relationships. Known for their freewheeling style, the hosts are often lampooned in late-night sketches.

programme.title: RaceWeek
programme.desc: Auto racing is one of the biggest spectator sports in America, with a number of organizations holding races nearly year-round so there's a lot to cover on "RaceWeek," which reports on the latest news from the racing world. Covering everything from NASCAR and IRL to Formula One and drag racing, the weekly show keeps viewers in the know with what's happening on their favorite circuits. In addition to offering the latest headlines, the show takes viewers behind the scenes to see what goes on with their favorite drivers, pit crews and team owners between races.
